title: AgentChaos — reliability testing for tool-using AI agents

scenes.caption: AgentChaos
scenes.vo: Ship a change to your A.I. agent's prompt, model, or retrieval, and you often won't know what broke until it's in production.

scenes.caption: A reliability gate in your CI
scenes.vo: AgentChaos is a reliability gate for your agent's C.I. It runs your scenarios on every pull request, and it never scores answer quality.

scenes.caption: Cost regression, caught
scenes.vo: Bump your retrieval from five chunks to twelve, and cost quietly jumps sixty-eight percent. AgentChaos catches the regression and fails the run.

scenes.caption: Names the root cause
scenes.vo: Then it names the cause, correlating every metric delta with what actually changed in the trace, right down to the metadata.

scenes.caption: Seeded chaos proxy
scenes.vo: It also injects seeded chaos between your agent and its tools.

scenes.caption: Graceful degradation = PASS
scenes.vo: A guaranteed five-oh-three on get order, and the agent degrades gracefully instead of crashing. That's a pass.

scenes.caption: Same seed, same faults
scenes.vo: Same seed, same faults, every single run. Fully reproducible.

scenes.caption: One gate, distinct exit codes
scenes.vo: In C.I. it's one gate. Clean changes pass; cost regressions and broken fallbacks get blocked by exit code, before they ship.

scenes.caption: AgentChaos — reliability for tool-using agents
scenes.vo: AgentChaos. Reliability testing for tool-using agents.
